Data Representation.title: Data Representation
Data Representation.content:
Decimal Number System

The decimal system is key for understanding how computers represent numbers and how to convert between bases.

Binary Number System

The binary system is used by computers internally, with only 0s and 1s to represent information.

Hexadecimal System

Hexadecimal is a compact way to write binary data, using 16 symbols from 0 to F. It is often used in programming and computer science to simplify large binary values.

Two's Complement

Two's complement is the standard method computers use to represent negative integers in binary form, making arithmetic operations easier for CPU circuits.

Algorithms.title: Algorithms
Algorithms.content:
Pseudocode

Pseudocode is a plain-language way to describe an algorithm step by step before writing actual code.

Flowchart Symbols

Flowchart symbols visually map the flow of an algorithm using shapes for decisions, processes, and start/end points.

Algorithm Efficiency

Efficiency measures how quickly an algorithm runs and how much memory it uses. Simple comparisons help choose the best approach for a specific problem.

Iteration and Conditionals

Most algorithms use loops and conditional checks to process data repeatedly and make decisions based on current values.
